lens.included_activities: US lower-middle-market custom roll formers that repeatedly engineer, tool, and produce metal profiles for external construction, transportation, appliance, storage, energy, industrial, and component customers.
lens.excluded_activities: Captive in-house forming lines, firms primarily selling standard proprietary products rather than outsourced custom work, machine builders, pure distributors, non-control financing vehicles, and operations that cannot transfer independently of the owner.
lens.customer_and_revenue_model: Recurring production runs and release orders priced per part, foot, pound, run, or program, often with separate or amortized engineering and tooling charges and customer-owned specifications.

a.bridge: BLS measures the broader NAICS 332 fabricated-metal workforce, not wage-weighted AI task exposure in custom roll forming. In May 2023, metal/plastic workers were 28.52% of NAICS 332 employment, assemblers/fabricators 10.84%, welders 8.17%, machinists 7.10%, cutting and press operators 4.01%, and CNC operators/programmers 5.17%. I assign little language-model substitution to machine setup, material handling, tooling, maintenance, inspection, and packing, while assigning higher exposure to quoting, drawing/specification review, engineering documentation, production planning, purchasing, quality records, and customer service. Custom roll formers may have more engineering and estimating work than the broad sector, so the proxy may understate exposure.
a.rationale: The production core is physical and precision-dependent, but every custom profile creates repeat document workflows around drawings, tolerances, material, tooling, quotes, change orders, schedules, certificates, and quality exceptions that AI can partially automate.
a.change_evidence: A wage-weighted task-time study from 10-15 independent roll formers, split among engineering, tooling, setup, production, quality, logistics, sales, and administration and documenting existing automation, would materially change the estimate.
caveats: Occupation evidence is for all fabricated metal manufacturing, not 332114.
Occupational employment is not a direct measure of AI substitutability or current automation.

rho.rationale: Drawing intake, assisted estimating, routing and schedule recommendations, purchase planning, certificate handling, and quality-document drafting are feasible within five years. Legacy ERP, small datasets, engineering liability, revision control, customer approvals, cyber requirements, and the need for physical trial and inspection constrain implementation.
rho.change_evidence: Plant deployments reporting sustained labor hours, quote cycle time, engineering-change errors, schedule adherence, material yield, quality escapes, model exceptions, and user adoption would materially update this range.
caveats: This is bounded judgment rather than an observed five-year adoption rate.
Conventional CNC and automation gains are excluded unless AI is the operative substitution mechanism.

e.rationale: Custom roll forming is inherently outsourced, specification-driven repeat production, so many independent firms fit the lens. Captive lines, proprietary-product manufacturers miscoded to the industry, firms outside the true EBITDA band, severe customer concentration, obsolete equipment, and owner-dependent engineering or sales reduce eligibility.
e.change_evidence: A verified list of the 115 dataset-estimated candidates with ownership, custom-versus-proprietary revenue, recurring programs, normalized EBITDA, customer concentration, equipment, and owner dependence would replace this estimate.
caveats: The provided n relies on receipts and a machinery-industry margin bridge rather than observed EBITDA.
A single large customer program can make an otherwise transferable plant economically fragile.

s5.rationale: A mature private industrial supplier base should generate retirement, strategic, and sponsor-led transfers, but long customer qualifications, equipment and tooling diligence, family succession, cyclicality, and concentration can delay or prevent qualifying control sales.
s5.change_evidence: A denominator-matched ten-year transaction set from PitchBook, Capital IQ, GF Data, Axial, and metal-fabrication advisers, paired with owner-age and succession-plan data for eligible firms, would materially change the probability.
caveats: No comprehensive six-digit control-transfer series was found.
Asset auctions, customer-program transfers, and internal reorganizations must be excluded.

q.rationale: Customer-specific tooling, approved profiles, integration into downstream products, and switching qualification support retention of savings, especially between program repricings. Metal-index clauses, annual cost-down demands, competitive rebids, open-book automotive practices, and customer purchasing leverage pass part of recurring benefit through.
q.change_evidence: Program contracts, metal pass-through and productivity clauses, annual cost-down terms, bid histories, switching/qualification timelines, and margin bridges after process improvements would establish actual capture.
caveats: No source directly measures retention of AI-derived savings.
Capture likely differs materially between engineered low-volume profiles and high-volume commodity sections.

d5.rationale: Roll-formed profiles serve durable physical uses across roofing, siding, gutters, solar, vehicles, storage, appliances, and industrial assemblies. Construction and manufacturing cycles, lightweighting, imports, alternative extrusion or stamping processes, and customer redesign create downside, while infrastructure, electrification, and modular construction can support growth.
d5.change_evidence: Constant-price Census shipments by product/end market, customer program forecasts, ABI construction data, vehicle and appliance production schedules, and EIA/DOE solar installation forecasts mapped to roll-formed content would materially change the range.
caveats: No complete constant-price, constant-quality forecast was found for custom roll forming.
BLS operator projections mix demand, productivity, and process substitution.

o.rationale: Remaining demand is for precisely formed physical metal profiles and requires accountable engineering, tooling, machine setup, material control, production, inspection, and delivery. Software can automate administrative and analytical steps but cannot supply the part; customer insourcing or alternate fabrication shifts the operator rather than digitizing the output.
o.change_evidence: Customer sourcing interviews and lost-order data quantifying insourcing, offshore supply, extrusion/stamping substitution, additive manufacturing, and standardized catalog replacement would materially change this value.
caveats: Domestic operators can still be displaced by imports or customer-owned lines.
Process substitution belongs here only when it removes the outsourced roll-forming operator for remaining end demand.

narrative.executive_view: Custom roll forming is a durable, specification-driven outsourced manufacturing niche with a small AI-addressable office and engineering layer. AI can improve quote-to-production and quality-document workflows, but the low supplied labor share and highly physical production core limit total labor opportunity.
narrative.ai_impact: AI can parse drawings and RFQs, compare revisions, assist cost estimates and routing, recommend schedules and purchases, draft certificates and corrective actions, and organize customer communication. Tool design approval, line setup, coil handling, forming, welding, cutting, inspection, maintenance, and physical release remain accountable plant work.
narrative.value_capture: Customer-specific tooling, approvals, and integration into downstream products create switching friction that can protect workflow gains. Contractual metal pass-through, annual cost downs, rebids, and concentrated procurement organizations will share some benefits over five years.
narrative.firm_availability: The supplied dataset estimates 115 firms in the economic band, but actual eligibility requires verifying custom external work, independence, normalized margins, customer concentration, equipment condition, and whether technical and sales relationships transfer beyond the owner.
narrative.demand_durability: Physical profiles remain essential in construction and manufactured products, with potential support from solar, infrastructure, and modular systems. Cyclicality, imports, customer insourcing, redesign, and alternate forming processes are the main threats rather than software elimination.
narrative.risks_and_uncertainty: Key uncertainties are the six-digit occupation mix, very low supplied compensation ratio, firm-level margin and ownership, customer and end-market concentration, current ERP maturity, and real end-market volumes. The supplied inputs mix wage and receipt vintages and apply a machinery margin bridge.
narrative.principal_driver: Repeat engineered programs generate high-friction drawing, quote, revision, planning, and quality workflows that AI can streamline.
narrative.principal_weakness: The labor base is small relative to receipts and most of it operates physical equipment, tooling, materials, or inspection.

sources.title: Fabricated Metal Product Manufacturing - May 2023 OEWS Industry-Specific Occupational Employment and Wage Estimates
sources.supports: BLS reports NAICS 332 occupation shares including 28.52% metal/plastic workers, 10.84% assemblers/fabricators, 8.17% welding workers, 7.10% machinists, 4.01% cutting/press operators, and 5.17% CNC operators/programmers.

sources.title: Metal and Plastic Machine Workers
sources.supports: BLS states that metal and plastic machine workers set up and operate equipment that shapes materials, that labor-saving CNC tools and robots are expanding, and projects the occupation group to decline 7% from 2024 to 2034.

sources.title: 2022 Economic Census Metal Forging and Stamping Classification Form
sources.supports: Census identifies 332114 as custom roll forming metal products by rotary motion of rolls to bend or shape products.

sources.title: Project Profile: Acme Express
sources.supports: DOE describes roll forming as a manufacturing process used to produce gutters and metal roofing and siding, supporting the physical end-use and construction link.

sources.title: Occupational Projections and Worker Characteristics
sources.supports: BLS projects rolling machine setter/operator/tender employment from 22,500 in 2024 to 20,600 in 2034, an 8% decline, while extruding and drawing machine employment is projected to rise 1%; used only as broad labor and technology context.
